A regression test for issue 340 in the JerryScript engine.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates a simple JavaScript expression that concatenates a null character (`'\0'`) with the string `'456'`. The result of this operation is a new string that starts with the null character followed by the characters '4', '5', and '6'. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily illustrating string concatenation involving a special character. It is a short script that can be used to test or demonstrate the behavior of string operations in JavaScript.
